title: cerastium latifolium subsp. uniflorum
intro: Cerastium latifolium subsp. uniflorum is a subspecies of the broad-leaved mouse-ear chickweed plant, known for its distinctive, single-flowered spikes. This plant is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its high content of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cerastium latifolium subsp. uniflorum include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
